title: Public Cache Boundary Audit
summary: Makes public read fetches revalidate-friendly and decouples contact/introduction/resume admin affordances from server-side session checks.
changed: Added shared public content revalidate fetch options.
Added public server API base helper that avoids next/headers when an absolute API origin is configured.
Changed public read helpers from no-store to next revalidate.
Moved contact/introduction/resume admin affordance checks to client-side gate.

intentionallyDynamicRoutes./blog: Search/pagination, local QA hooks, and server-side admin create affordance remain.
intentionallyDynamicRoutes./blog/[slug]: Server-side admin detail payload loading remains for inline editing.
intentionallyDynamicRoutes./works: Search/pagination, local QA hooks, and server-side admin create affordance remain.
intentionallyDynamicRoutes./works/[slug]: Server-side admin detail payload loading remains for inline editing.

validations.command: npx vitest run src/test/public-api-clients.test.ts src/test/public-api-contracts.test.ts src/test/public-admin-rendering.test.tsx src/test/public-admin-client-gate.test.tsx --pool=threads
focused Playwright via local dev server
validations.result: blocked by unavailable Docker/nginx API target at 127.0.0.1:3000

risks: Default Docker build without absolute API origin still marks public routes dynamic to avoid requiring a live backend at image build time.
Client-side admin gates hydrate after initial page render.
Blog and works server-side admin affordances remain to be decoupled in a later pass.
recommendation: Proceed with this focused public read caching boundary improvement; handle blog/works admin affordance decoupling separately.
